verdict: 2026-07-13 15:25 PDT — The server teardown and overall documentation shape are sound; manifests are unchanged, deleted machinery is not presented as current, and the SVG copies are byte-identical. One categorical storage claim remains, so the doc set is not yet fully reconciled.

findings.title: Incident policy still categorically claims no user data is persisted
findings.claim: “The app persists no user data” is unqualified and conflicts with the unchanged `/api/log` sink plus Railway retention. The same policy now recognizes exposure of operational logs as an incident, making the contradiction internal as well as inconsistent with Privacy and the other security policies.
findings.alternative: Scope the statement to the removed store: “The app persists no presenter configuration or per-user record, so there is no presenter-data encryption key to rotate.” Preserve the operational-log exception.
findings.win: Eliminates the final false no-user-data guarantee while retaining the accurate reason no rate-store encryption key exists.
